checks.check: A — Substance floor (bullet diff vs canonical)
checks.evidence: All 19 bullets present and intact vs canonical resume.json. All metrics preserved: 25+ stakeholders, 70+ staff, 75% onboarding reduction, 600+ participants, 78% client conversion rate, 20+/10+ programs/vendors, 2,000+ employee experiences, 5 cross-functional projects, 12/23 DEI locations, 3 global locations, 566% applications, 300+ leave cases, $362K leave costs. Key scope clauses intact: 'positioning, delivery strategy, and operations'; 'improving program knowledge'; 'client conversion rate'; 'award-winning well-being initiatives recognized among 1,000+ competitors'. No bullet dropped or thinned.

ODN correctly labeled 'Consultant, Organization & Talent Development' at 'Organization Development Network (ODN) Oregon' — NOT community/ERG. Bullets describe leave-cost diagnosis (300+ cases, $362K) and NGO focus-group/accountability work only. No invented tools: skills block matches content_library.md canonical vocabulary exactly (Rippling/ADP/SAP/Greenhouse/Canvas/Moodle/Asana/Airtable/SPSS/Notion/SharePoint/Canva/Adobe InDesign/GenAI Tools). No '230+/80+/top-3-univ' Kronos fabrication. No '$340K/17 launches' composite. '78% client conversion rate' used (sales-oriented EX role — within allowed variant).

checks.check: C — Bullet counts (ODN 2–3; all others exactly 4)
checks.evidence: ODN: 3 bullets (within 2–3 range, both projects covered: absenteeism/leave-cost in bullets 1–2, NGO leadership in bullet 3). InGenius: 4 bullets. NextGen: 4 bullets. Vestas: 4 bullets. Kronos: 4 bullets. Total = 19 bullets. No bullets deleted.

checks.check: D — Cover substance + voice (metrics, bolds ≥6, org-psych close, salutation)
checks.evidence: All canonical metrics bolded in cover body: '3 global locations', '2,000+ employee experiences', '20+ programs and 10+ vendors', '70+ staff that cut onboarding time by 75%', '600+ participants', '78% program enrollment rate', '25+ global stakeholders'. 30+ body bolds total including ≥1 company (Vestas Wind Systems, NextGen Healthcare, InGenius Prep, Xenium HR) and ≥1 metric-with-number. Org-psych scientific-method close present: 'MS in Applied Organizational Psychology (USC)...diagnose with data, collaborate cross-functionally on solutions, track metrics to ensure real progress'. 'analytical organizer with a growth mindset' present. Salutation: 'Dear Xenium HR Hiring Team,' — correct. No visa mention. No relocation over-commitment. No clichés or JD-copy-paste.

checks.check: E — Header location + Wesleyan Relevant Coursework
checks.evidence: Header: 'Portland, OR' — Xenium HR Tualatin is Portland metro, Portland, OR is correct for a local/hybrid role. Wesleyan Relevant Coursework present: 'Educational Psychology, Practicum in Education Studies, Psychological Testing, Learning and Motivation'. USC Relevant Coursework also present: 'Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology'.

Belief opener confirmed (NOT anecdote/scene): 'Employee experience isn't just about answering inquiries—from my experience, it's about creating a continuous cycle of collecting insights, analyzing data, and refining solutions so that every employee who reaches out feels genuinely supported.' Tagline line 2 is the approved EX/People Ops variant: 'Dedicated to Improving People/Employee Experience'. Paragraph order: Vestas → NextGen → InGenius — correct per canonical skeleton; no ODN paragraph (appropriate for EX rep role, not OD-diagnostic). Vestas paragraph: M&A sentence present verbatim ('Overseeing the integration of an acquired company during an M&A project further demonstrated my ability to manage organizational change and keep cross-functional teams aligned.'). InGenius paragraph: canonical close present verbatim ('I run needs assessments with 25+ global stakeholders, translating ambiguous asks into structured programs, and apply continuous feedback loops to refine program positioning, delivery strategy, and operations.'). Org-psych close and 'analytical organizer with a growth mindset' present. NOTE: NextGen para uses 'data analysis and advisory skills' (drops 'strategic planning' from canonical 3-part phrase) — this is within the allowed keyword-swap of bolded skill phrases for an EX-focused role; the underlying work is true and no substance is fabricated. No free-written sentences detected outside the {SWAP} slots.
